steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts the road-map method (described as 'not very accurate') with Hosseini's program, which 'more accurately identified sidewalks' and 'even distinguished between concrete and brick.' The blank must describe the program's performance relative to the road-map method.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘changed’: 'Changed' only indicates that something is different — it says nothing about whether the change was better or worse, and the sentence structure 'performance was changed to that of' is idiomatically off. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘superior’: 'Superior to' means higher in quality — exactly what the evidence (greater accuracy, finer distinction) supports. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘similar’: 'Similar' implies roughly equal performance, but the passage explicitly says the program was more accurate, not comparable. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘supportive’: 'Supportive of that of the road-map method' is idiomatically awkward and does not convey a performance comparison. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
